SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.datasource

Interface FileSource

public interface FileSource
Interface for creating file openers that can parse files of a specific format.

A FileSource is created by a FileFormat and is responsible for creating FileOpener instances that are bound to a particular schema and allocator. This level of indirection allows the opener to capture the schema and allocator without requiring them as parameters on every file open call.

Example: 

class TsvFileSource implements FileSource {
    @Override public FileOpener createFileOpener(
            Schema schema, BufferAllocator allocator, FileScanConfig scanConfig) {
        return new TsvFileOpener(schema, allocator);
    }
    @Override public String fileType() {
        return "tsv";
    }
}
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: FileSource

    FileOpener
    createFileOpener(org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema schema, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator, FileScanConfig scanConfig)
    Creates a FileOpener bound to the given schema, allocator, and scan configuration.
    String
    fileType()
    Returns a string identifier for this file source's type.

  • Method Details

    • createFileOpener

      FileOpener createFileOpener(org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema schema, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator, FileScanConfig scanConfig)
      Creates a FileOpener bound to the given schema, allocator, and scan configuration.

      The scan configuration provides query-specific parameters such as column projection, row limit, batch size, and partition index that implementations can use to optimize file reading.

      Example: 

      FileSource source = myFormat.fileSource();
FileOpener opener = source.createFileOpener(
    schema, allocator, scanConfig);
RecordBatchReader reader = opener.open(
    partitionedFile);
      Parameters:
      schema - the expected output schema
      allocator - allocator for Arrow vectors
      scanConfig - scan configuration with projection, limit, batch size, and partition info
      Returns:
      a FileOpener that can parse file content into record batches
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: FileSource::create_file_opener

    • fileType

      String fileType()
      Returns a string identifier for this file source's type.

      This is used by DataFusion for display and logging purposes. Common values include format names like "csv", "json", "parquet", etc.

      Example: 

      FileSource source = myFormat.fileSource();
String type = source.fileType(); // e.g., "tsv"
System.out.println("File source type: " + type);
      Returns:
      the file type identifier
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: FileSource::file_type
